title: Panel — one visual component that renders one layer
description: A panel renders exactly ONE layer (its primary layer_id). The librarian's page_composition_plan_v1 reports the panel_state; the panel renderer reads the layer payload and the panel_state to decide what to show. Per ADAPTER_CONTRACT.md §4. Panels never produce layers themselves; producing is an analysis adapter's job.

layer_id.description: The layer this panel renders. FK into layer_registry.layer_id.

optional_layers.description: Other layers the panel can overlay / decorate with when available.

renderer.description: Hint to the page about how to lay out the panel. 'custom' means the producing adapter's preview() is the renderer.

preview_via_adapter.description: If true, the page calls the producing adapter's preview(layer_payload, panel_id) instead of using a built-in renderer.
